title: Seed Doesn’t Tell the Whole Story
text:
Every March, the NCAA tournament captivates millions. But how much does a team’s seed really tell us about their chances?
Since 1985, 1-seeds have won roughly 55% of championships. That means 45% of the time, someone else cuts down the nets. Seeds are a starting point, not a destiny.

histData = [
  {seed: "1", pct: 55},
  {seed: "2", pct: 17},
  {seed: "3", pct: 10},
  {seed: "4-5", pct: 8},
  {seed: "6-8", pct: 7},
  {seed: "9+", pct: 3}
]

Plot.plot({
  height: 300,
  x: {label: "Seed", domain: ["1", "2", "3", "4-5", "6-8", "9+"]},
  y: {label: "% of Championships (1985-2024)", domain: [0, 60]},
  marks: [
    Plot.barY(histData, {x: "seed", y: "pct", fill: "#4a90d9", tip: true}),
    Plot.ruleY([0])
  ],
  style: {fontSize: 14}
})















Historical championship distribution by seed, 1985-2024. Data: NCAA.
